prior.content_md:
**Open Burning Ban — No Registration; Compliance by Abstention**
The Summer Open Burning Ban imposes no registration or permit requirement on businesses seeking to comply — the obligation is simply to refrain from prohibited burning activities. There is no filing, no permit number, and no renewal cycle. The workspace must ensure that operational SOPs, subcontractor contracts, and facility management policies affirmatively prohibit prohibited burning categories (yard debris, land-clearing debris, acquired structure burns, weed abatement, storm vegetative debris) at all Georgia sites in covered counties during May 1 – September 30. No reporting to EPD is required absent a violation.

**Drought Response — Public Water System Obligations (Indirect)**
Direct reporting and information campaign obligations under Drought Response Level 1 attach to **public water systems**, not to freight forwarders or private operators. The workspace's data-collection obligation is limited to: (a) identifying which Georgia facilities are served by public water systems versus private wells; (b) tracking whether a variance has been applied for by the serving utility (public water systems must obtain a variance from EPD to impose restrictions different from state requirements); and (c) monitoring EPD's drought reports, which are published on a regular basis at the EPD Drought Management page. EPD gathers input from potentially affected permittees over a three-business-day period prior to any new drought declaration level.

**NPDES Construction Stormwater — Notice of Intent (NOI) Filing Required**
For any construction project disturbing one or more acres at a Georgia location, the workspace as permit operator must:
- Register for and submit through the **Georgia EPD Online System (GEOS)** (epd.georgia.gov/geos/)
- File a **Notice of Intent (NOI)** before any ground disturbance begins
- Maintain a **Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP)** on site
- File a **Notice of Termination (NOT)** upon project completion and stabilization

All submittals must be made electronically through GEOS; paper submissions are not accepted. The current NPDES General Permits (GAR100001, GAR100002, GAR100003) are in effect through **July 31, 2028**. Data fields required for NOI include: site address, county, disturbed acreage, receiving water body, project type, and responsible official designation.

*Source: NPDES Construction Stormwater General Permits, Georgia EPD. https://epd.georgia.gov/forms-permits/watershed-protection-branch-forms-permits/storm-water-forms/npdes-construction; Drought Management, Georgia EPD. https://epd.georgia.gov/watershed-protection-branch/drought-management.*
